growth_records.strain: Species A (co-culture with Ramlibacter lithotrophicus Species B)

up.units: mol Mn(II) cell^-1 h^-1 (combined co-culture, per total cell)
up.method: ICP-MS time-course of acid-soluble Mn(II) vs qPCR cell counts
derived from per-cell rate and biomass yield

sec.units: mol Mn cell^-1 h^-1 (1:1 with Mn(II) oxidized)

yields.Yxs_biomass: ~100-200 mg dry biomass per mol Mn(II) oxidized (co-culture; estimated from E. coli single-cell dry weight)
yields.cell_yield: 7.4e11 total cells per mol Mn(II) oxidized (Species A 6.4e11 + Species B 1.0e11)
yields.DNA_yield: 3.1e6 ng DNA per mol Mn(II) oxidized

growth_records.cite: Yu et al. Bacterial chemolithoautotrophy via manganese oxidation. Nature (2020). DOI:10.1038/s41586-020-2468-5. [PMC7802741]
growth_records.snippet: The co-culture oxidised Mn(II) at a combined rate of 3.4 to 9.0 e-15 mol Mn(II) cell-1 hr-1. ... 6.4e11 cells of Species A, 1.0e11 cells of Species B, for a combined yield of 7.4e11 cells (mol Mn(II) oxidised)-1 ... growth yield ~100-200 mg
growth_records.notes: Autotrophic Mn(II)-oxidizing chemolithotroph grown ONLY as a 2-species co-culture (Species A is the dominant Mn oxidizer, ~7:1). Growth is extremely slow. Species A doubling time Td=6.1 day (reported). mu=ln2/Td = 0.693/(6.1*24 h)=0.693/146.4=0.00473 /h. Mn(II) uptake reported per cell (3.4-9.0e-15 mol/cell/h, combined). Biomass-normalized qMn derived: gDW/cell = biomass yield / cell yield = (0.1-0.2 g/mol)/(7.4e11 cells/mol) = 1.35e-13 to 2.70e-13 gDW/cell; qMn = rate/(gDW/cell): low 3.4e-15/2.70e-13=0.0126 mol/gDW/h=12.6 mmol/gDW/h; high 9.0e-15/1.35e-13=0.0667=66.7 mmol/gDW/h -> ~13-67 mmol Mn/gDW/h (approximate, wide). O2 uptake not directly measured; stoichiometry 0.5 mol O2 per mol Mn(II) from Mn(II)+0.5O2+H2O->MnO2+2H+. CO2/bicarbonate fixation confirmed qualitatively by 13C nanoSIMS (rTCA cycle in Species A). Buffered pH 6.5-6.8 (used 6.65 midpoint); unbuffered 5.7-6.3. Temp optimum 34-40C, grown at 37C.
